obligations.reason: The scope exposes JTAG pins and DMI/JTAG/SimJTAG instances plus debug request/master signals; verify that a debug/test subject cannot control protected debug capabilities without authorization.
obligations.subject: external JTAG/SimJTAG/SimDTM debug requester
obligations.operation: trigger debug entry, reset, read/write via debug module
obligations.object: debug module controls and system access signals: debug_req_o, ndmreset_o, dmactive_o, dm_master_* and dm_slave_*
obligations.expected_guard: debug authentication/authorization (e.g., jtag_unlock or equivalent debug enable) before DMI/JTAG can issue debug requests, memory transactions, ndmreset, or debug_req_o
obligations.candidate_path: JTAG/DTM/DMI path through riscv_peripherals to debug module master/slave signals
required_evidence_slots: operation: debug request/reset or memory read/write
observed RTL behavior and source lines
knowledge_used: evidence closure: subject, operation, object, guard, reachability
obligations.uncertainty: Need inspect actual assignments/instance connections because fact layer lists signals and instances but not guards.

obligations.reason: reglk_wrapper has a jtag_unlock input and outputs lock controls used by multiple wrappers; verify JTAG cannot bypass sticky write protection without an authorization guard.
obligations.subject: JTAG/debug requester
obligations.operation: unlock, clear, or override register lock controls
obligations.object: reglk_ctrl_o / reglk_ctrl lock state controlling peripheral register protections
obligations.expected_guard: jtag_unlock should itself be authenticated and should not clear/override lock bits except under authorized lifecycle/debug state
obligations.candidate_path: jtag_unlock generation and use from riscv_peripherals into reglk_wrapper
state_conditions_to_check: whether unlock is sticky or transient
required_evidence_slots: object: reglk_ctrl_o and downstream reglk_ctrl_i guards
observed behavior at generation and use site
source locations in top and wrapper
obligations.uncertainty: The fact layer identifies the input but not whether unlock is protected or how lock bits update.

obligations.reason: AES wrappers expose AXI register interfaces and contain key/state signals with reglk_ctrl_i and debug_mode_i inputs; check lock enforcement at actual write enables.
obligations.subject: software or bus master accessing AES MMIO
obligations.object: AES key registers/signals key0/key1/key2/key_reg*, cii_K/core_key, state/control/start registers
obligations.expected_guard: writes to AES key/config/start registers should be blocked when corresponding reglk_ctrl_i bits indicate locked; all write paths including debug_mode_i paths should use the guard
obligations.candidate_path: reglk_ctrl_i use in AES wrappers for key/control/data registers
required_evidence_slots: expected guard: reglk_ctrl_i bit and any access-control bit
source lines for decode and register update
obligations.uncertainty: Exact address map and lock-bit mapping require source inspection.

obligations.reason: The wrappers contain secret key/internal state signals and AXI rdata outputs; verify read decode does not expose protected material without guard.
obligations.subject: software or bus master reading AES MMIO
obligations.object: AES key material and internal state: key0/key1/key2/key_reg*, key_big/cii_K/core_key, state/state_big/core_block; read data rdata
obligations.expected_guard: secret key/internal state registers should not be readable, or reads should be mediated by lock/privilege/debug authorization; ciphertext/result reads should not expose stale secret-dependent state beyond intended output
obligations.candidate_path: AES read muxes from key/state/result registers to AXI rdata and NoC response
required_evidence_slots: expected guard: non-readable or lock/privilege authorization
external path: rdata to ariane_axi output/NoC response
obligations.uncertainty: Need source read case statements to distinguish intended result registers from secret registers.

obligations.reason: HMAC wrapper has key/control signals and reglk_ctrl_i/acct_ctrl_i/debug_mode_i inputs; verify protected control and key updates are mediated.
obligations.subject: software or bus master accessing HMAC MMIO
obligations.operation: write/configure/start HMAC or bypass key hashing
obligations.object: HMAC key/control registers/signals including key0, key_hash_bypass, startHash/newMessage, data
obligations.expected_guard: HMAC key and bypass/control configuration writes should be gated by reglk_ctrl_i/acct_ctrl_i and not writable by unauthorized AXI subjects once locked
obligations.candidate_path: HMAC wrapper key/data/control register writes and key_hash_bypass
required_evidence_slots: expected guard: reglk_ctrl_i/acct_ctrl_i and no debug bypass unless authorized
obligations.uncertainty: Need inspect wrapper register update logic and lock-bit mapping.

obligations.reason: Fact layer lists HMAC key and derived hash byte signals near AXI rdata; check whether read mux exposes protected material.
obligations.subject: software or bus master reading HMAC MMIO
obligations.object: HMAC key0, ikey_hash_bytes, okey_hash_bytes, internal hash state/data, rdata
obligations.expected_guard: HMAC keys, inner/outer key hash bytes, and intermediate state should not be externally readable except through intended digest/status under authorization
obligations.candidate_path: HMAC read muxes and outputs exposing key/intermediate hash state
obligations.uncertainty: Signal list is truncated; inspect full module for rdata case statement and output registers.

obligations.reason: The lock controller is central to downstream protection; verify lock bits cannot be rewritten or reset to an unsafe state through normal MMIO or peripheral resets.
obligations.subject: software/MMIO requester and reset control path
obligations.operation: write, set, clear, reset lock state
obligations.object: register lock state reglk_ctrl_o/reglk_ctrl and reset controls rst_9/rst_ni
obligations.expected_guard: lock bits should have safe reset defaults, become effective at all downstream use sites, and be sticky or only cleared by authorized reset/lifecycle/debug unlock
obligations.candidate_path: reglk_wrapper register update and reset behavior
state_conditions_to_check: locked state followed by attempted clear
required_evidence_slots: subject: AXI/MMIO requester or reset controller
expected guard: sticky semantics or authorized clear only
obligations.uncertainty: Need source to determine reset polarity/default and if lock bits are write-once.

obligations.reason: acct_wrapper outputs access control bits for peripherals; verify the policy registers themselves are protected from unauthorized modification.
obligations.subject: software or bus master accessing accounting/access-control MMIO
obligations.object: access-control policy acc_ctrl_o/acc_ctrl and acct_ctrl_i/reglk_ctrl_i guarded registers
obligations.expected_guard: updates to access-control matrix acc_ctrl_o should require authorization and/or lock; once configured/locked, unauthorized writes should not change peripheral access permissions
obligations.candidate_path: acct_wrapper access-control policy programming and acc_ctrl distribution
obligations.uncertainty: Need inspect how acct_ctrl_i and we_flag gate writes.

obligations.reason: riscv_peripherals has many NoC ports and acc_ctrl/reglk_ctrl signals; verify address decoding and routing enforce the access-control policy uniformly.
obligations.subject: NoC/AXI bus master reaching peripheral wrappers
obligations.operation: route read/write request to protected peripheral
obligations.expected_guard: requests to protected peripherals (AES/HMAC/SHA/reglk/acct/reset/debug) should be mediated by access-control policy acc_ctrl, privilege/debug state, and correct address-range checks
obligations.candidate_path: top-level peripheral routing / NoC address decode to crypto and control wrappers
required_evidence_slots: source lines for request and response path
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer lacks request decode details; inspect top-level unique case around line ~811 and connections.

obligations.reason: Top-level exposes multiple per-peripheral reset signals connected to protected crypto and policy wrappers; check reset authorization and post-reset defaults.
obligations.subject: software/bus requester controlling reset peripheral or reset registers
obligations.operation: reset/clear protected peripheral or policy state
obligations.object: peripheral reset signals rst_1/rst_2/rst_3/rst_4/rst_6/rst_9/rst_11 and protected state in wrappers
obligations.expected_guard: software-controlled or bus-controlled resets of crypto, lock, accounting, and debug-related blocks should require authorization and should not clear locks/secrets in a way that reopens protected access without policy mediation
obligations.candidate_path: reset-control registers/signals rst_1..rst_13 distributed to security peripherals
state_conditions_to_check: rst_ni global reset vs per-block rst_*
required_evidence_slots: object: protected peripheral state or policy/lock state
expected guard: authorized reset and safe default
obligations.uncertainty: Need identify source of rst_* assignments in top-level.

obligations.reason: SHA256 wrapper has AXI interface with reglk_ctrl_i/acct_ctrl_i and hash core instance; verify protected register mediation and no unintended internal-state exposure.
obligations.subject: software or bus master accessing SHA256 MMIO
obligations.object: SHA256 message/control/internal state and rdata output
obligations.expected_guard: SHA control/message registers should be mediated by acct_ctrl_i/reglk_ctrl_i as applicable; internal hash state should not be exposed except intended digest/status
obligations.candidate_path: SHA256 wrapper register access and result/state readback
required_evidence_slots: expected guard: acct_ctrl_i/reglk_ctrl_i for protected writes and no secret/internal read exposure
obligations.uncertainty: Hash data may not be secret in all uses; focus on access-policy and unintended exposure paths.

obligations.reason: Top-level includes DMA NoC ingress/egress ports alongside protected peripheral ports; check that DMA cannot access crypto/control/debug/register-lock regions without mediation.
obligations.subject: DMA engine / DMA NoC port
obligations.operation: DMA read/write to protected MMIO/peripheral region
obligations.object: protected peripheral address ranges and registers reachable from buf_ariane_dma_noc2_data_i / ariane_dma_buf_noc3_data_o
obligations.expected_guard: DMA-originated requests should be subject to the same access-control/address-range policy as CPU requests and should not bypass acc_ctrl/reglk/privilege checks
obligations.candidate_path: DMA NoC path to protected peripheral address ranges
state_conditions_to_check: configuration-update race if acc_ctrl changes during transfer
obligations.uncertainty: Need inspect top-level decode and whether DMA is a distinct requester or only a peripheral endpoint.
